title: mentha × verticillata f. peduncularis
intro: Mentha × verticillata f. peduncularis, also known as ginger mint or bearded mint, is a hybrid species of the mint plant family, characterized by its distinctive long stems and bearded appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and headaches, and reduce fever. The health benefits of ginger mint include its ability to improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its role in reducing stress and promoting better sleep. Ginger mint can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health issues, or added to food and drinks for its unique flavor and aroma.
